# Metal Roof Cost Broward County 2026: Installation Prices

**Quick Answer:** The cost to install metal roof in Broward County 2026 ranges from **$25,000 to $55,000** for most single-family homes, with the average project landing around **$37,500**. This price reflects the premium materials, strict HVHZ building codes, and labor rates specific to the Fort Lauderdale and Coral Springs markets.

Many homeowners search for the **cost to install metal roof in Broward County 2026** because they want durability against hurricanes and long-term energy savings. The investment is higher than asphalt shingles, but the lifespan and wind ratings justify the expense for South Florida properties.

---

## Average Cost Breakdown

| Cost Level | Price Range | Typical Scope |
|-----------|------------|---------------|
| Budget | **$25,000 – $32,000** | 26-gauge, 1,500 sq ft, simple gable |
| Mid-Range | **$32,000 – $42,000** | 24-gauge, 2,000 sq ft, standing seam |
| High-End | **$42,000 – $55,000+** | 22-gauge, custom design, complex hips |

**Budget** options typically feature painted steel panels in a standard color palette. They meet minimum code requirements but offer less aesthetic customization.

**Mid-range** covers the most common choice for Broward homeowners — 24-gauge standing seam with hidden fasteners. This system provides better wind uplift resistance and cleaner lines.

**High-end** includes thicker gauge metal, custom colors, integrated snow guards (rare but possible), and complex roof geometries with multiple valleys and penetrations.

These estimates include materials, labor, disposal, and standard permitting fees. They do not account for structural reinforcement if your existing trusses cannot support the new load.

---

## What Affects the Cost in Broward County

### 1. Roof Size

Roofing contractors price metal roofs based on the total square footage of the roof deck, not the floor plan. A typical Broward home has a roof between 1,500 and 2,500 sq ft. At **$16.00–$22.00 per sq ft** installed, a 1,500 sq ft roof runs roughly **$25,000–$30,000** while a 2,500 sq ft roof pushes **$40,000–$50,000**.

### 2. Material Gauge and Type

The thickness of the metal dictates the price. Thinner panels are cheaper but less durable against hail or falling branches. 29-gauge steel is the entry point at **$14.00–$16.00 per sq ft**. 24-gauge standing seam is the standard for quality at **$18.00–$24.00 per sq ft**. Copper or zinc panels cost **$30.00–$45.00 per sq ft** but are rare in residential projects.

### 3. Labor Rates in Broward County

Roofing labor in Broward County runs **$60–$85 per hour**, roughly 20% above the national average. Skilled metal installers are in high demand due to the complexity of the work compared to asphalt. Specialized crews charge more because they understand HVHZ requirements and panel seaming techniques.

### 4. Tear-off and Disposal Costs

Disposal of the old roof is a line item you cannot skip. Broward County requires licensed waste facilities for roofing debris. Expect to pay **$1,500–$3,500** for tear-off and haul-away depending on the number of layers. If you have tile, the weight increases disposal fees significantly.

### 5. Permits and Inspections

Broward County requires a building permit for any roof replacement or new installation. Permit fees run **$400–$800** depending on project value and whether you are replacing a tile or metal roof. The process includes a pre-inspection and final inspection. Your contractor should pull the permit — if they suggest skipping it, walk away.

### 6. HVHZ Code Requirements

Broward County is inside the High Velocity Hurricane Zone. Every roofing product must carry a Miami-Dade NOA or Florida Product Approval. This limits material options and adds **$3,000–$6,000** compared to non-HVHZ areas. Fastener schedules are stricter too — specific screw types at tighter intervals than standard code.

---

## How the Cost to Install Metal Roof in Broward County 2026 is Calculated

Understanding the **cost to install metal roof in Broward County 2026** requires looking at the specific line items that make up the final quote. Contractors do not just add material and labor; they factor in code compliance, insurance, and overhead.

The base calculation starts with the roof area in squares (100 sq ft). You multiply the area by the material cost per square. Then you add labor hours based on complexity. A simple gable roof takes half the time of a complex hip roof.

Next, you add the permit fees charged by Broward County. This covers the administrative cost of reviewing the plans and scheduling the inspections. You also factor in the cost of waste disposal tickets for the local landfill or recycling center.

Finally, the contractor adds their profit margin and insurance costs. This includes general liability and workers compensation, which are higher in Florida due to litigation risks. The **cost to install metal roof in Broward County 2026** reflects all these layers combined into a single bid.

---

## Cost by Material Type

| Material | Cost per Sq Ft | Lifespan |
|---------|---------------|----------|
| 29-Gauge Steel | **$14.00 – $16.00** | 30–40 years |
| 24-Gauge Steel | **$18.00 – $24.00** | 40–50 years |
| Aluminum | **$20.00 – $28.00** | 50+ years |
| Stone-Coated | **$15.00 – $20.00** | 40–50 years |
| Copper | **$30.00 – $45.00** | 100+ years |
| Zinc | **$35.00 – $50.00** | 80+ years |

**29-gauge steel** is the budget-friendly option. It meets code but is thinner and more prone to oil-canning (rippling) under heat expansion. Best for rental properties or tight budgets.

**24-gauge steel** is the sweet spot. It is thick enough to resist hail and wind uplift without excessive oil-canning. This is what most Broward homeowners choose for longevity.

**Aluminum** is naturally rust-proof, which is ideal for coastal Broward homes near the Intracoastal or Atlantic Ocean. It resists salt corrosion better than steel but costs slightly more upfront.

**Stone-coated metal** looks like traditional shingles or tile but uses metal panels underneath. It appeals to homeowners who want the aesthetic of tile without the weight. Rated to 120 mph winds.

**Copper and zinc** are premium materials that develop a patina over time. They are extremely durable and can last a century. The **cost to install metal roof in Broward County 2026** for these materials is high, but they require little maintenance.

---

## Florida-Specific Considerations

**Building Codes:** Florida Building Code 8th Edition (2023) governs all roof replacements in Broward County. Key requirements include secondary water barrier on the entire roof deck, specific nail patterns for shingle attachment, and enhanced flashing at all penetrations. These go beyond what most states require.

**Hurricane Zone:** Broward County is in the HVHZ — the strictest wind zone in Florida. All roofing materials must hold a Miami-Dade NOA. Products are tested with missile impact tests and cyclic pressure tests that simulate hurricane conditions. Your contractor should provide NOA numbers for every product they plan to use.

**Permits:** Apply through Broward County's ePlan online portal or at the Building Department in Plantation. Turnaround is 5–10 business days for a standard residential permit. Budget **$400–$800** for the fee. Two inspections required: pre-cover and final.

**Insurance Impact:** This is the big one. A new roof resets the clock with your insurance company. Many Florida insurers won't write or renew policies on homes with roofs older than 15 years — some draw the line at 10. Upgrading to a hip roof shape or adding hurricane straps during replacement can qualify you for wind mitigation discounts of **15–45%** on your premium.

**HOA:** Many Broward communities restrict roofing material, color, and style. Metal roofs are generally allowed, but some deed-restricted communities require specific colors or profiles. Get written HOA approval before signing a contractor agreement — changing materials mid-job is extremely expensive.

**Season:** Best time to replace a roof here is December through April — dry season, lower humidity, fewer storm damage backlogs. Avoid peak hurricane season (August–October). Rainy season (May–October) brings daily afternoon thunderstorms that delay projects.

**My Safe Florida Home:** If your home was built before 2008 and is homesteaded, you may qualify for a free wind inspection and matching grants up to **$10,000** toward hurricane hardening improvements including roof upgrades.

---

## How to Save Money on Metal Roof Installation in Broward County

1.  **Get at least 4 written quotes.** Contractor pricing varies by 20–35% for identical scope in Broward County. On a **$37,000** job, that's **$7,500–$12,000** in potential savings. Make sure each quote itemizes materials, labor, tear-off, permits, and disposal separately.

2.  **Schedule in dry season.** December through April is slower for roofers. You'll have more leverage to negotiate and fewer weather delays. Some contractors offer **5–10%** off-season discounts for metal installation projects.

3.  **Choose 24-gauge over 22-gauge if budget allows.** The savings between 24-gauge (**$18.00–$24.00/sq ft**) and 22-gauge (**$25.00–$32.00/sq ft**) can be **$5,000–$8,000** on a typical home. 24-gauge is sufficient for HVHZ compliance in most residential zones.

4.  **Bundle hurricane upgrades with the roof.** Adding roof-to-wall straps or upgraded underlayment during a replacement is **40–60% cheaper** than standalone projects. The combined wind mitigation improvements can also cut your insurance premium by hundreds per year.

5.  **Check for insurance claim eligibility.** If your existing roof has storm damage, your insurance may cover part or all of the replacement. File the claim before signing a contractor agreement. Be cautious of contractors who offer to "handle the insurance" — work with your adjuster directly.

---

## When to Replace Your Roof — Warning Signs

**Your roof is 20–30 years old.** Metal roofs last longer than asphalt, but paint finishes degrade. A 30-year-old metal roof in South Florida has taken more UV abuse than a newer roof in the Midwest. Check for fading, chalking, or loose panels.

**Your insurance company is pressuring you.** A non-renewal notice or letter requesting a roof inspection means the clock is ticking. Many Florida insurers are actively dropping homes with aging roofs that lack current wind ratings.

**Visible rust or corrosion.** Check your gutters after heavy rain. Rust flakes in the water mean the protective coating has failed. Pitting on the surface means the metal is thinning and will eventually leak.

**Loose fasteners or oil-canning.** If you hear rattling during high winds, the screws may have backed out. Oil-canning (rippling) is mostly cosmetic but can indicate thermal expansion issues that compromise the seal.

**Interior water stains or attic moisture.** Brown ceiling spots, bubbling paint, or damp insulation point to roof leaks. In Broward's humidity, even small leaks lead to mold fast. Multiple leaks usually mean the whole system has failed.

---

## How to Hire a Roofing Contractor in Broward County

1.  **Verify their Florida license** at [MyFloridaLicense.com](https://www.myfloridalicense.com). You need a Certified Roofing Contractor (CCC), Registered Roofing Contractor (RC), or General Contractor (CGC) license.

2.  **Confirm active insurance.** Ask for a Certificate of Insurance showing general liability (minimum $1 million) and workers comp. Call the insurer directly to verify.

3.  **Check for a Broward County local business tax receipt.** This confirms they're registered locally, not a storm chaser from out of state.

4.  **Get 3–4 written estimates** with line-item breakdowns — material brand, underlayment type, number of squares, tear-off, permits, labor, timeline, and warranty.

5.  **Check reviews on Google and BBB.** Look for patterns in complaints, not just star ratings.

6.  **Never pay more than 10% upfront.** Florida Statute 489.126 restricts contractor deposits. Payment should tie to milestones: deposit, tear-off complete, mid-project, final inspection passed.

7.  **Get everything in writing.** Contract must include: scope, material specs with NOA numbers, dates, payment schedule, warranty terms, permit responsibility, and cleanup plan.

---

## Frequently Asked Questions

### How much does it cost to install metal roof in Broward County 2026?

Most homeowners pay between **$25,000 and $55,000**. The average is around **$37,500**. Your actual cost depends on roof size, material choice, complexity, and whether structural repairs are needed.

### Do I need a permit to install a metal roof in Broward County?

Yes, always. Apply through Broward County's ePlan portal or at the Building Department. Budget **$400–$800**. Two inspections required. Never let a contractor skip the permit — it can void your insurance and create title issues when you sell.

### How long does a metal roof installation take in Broward County?

Asphalt shingle roofs take **2–4 days** for an average home. Metal roofs take **5–10 days** due to the precision required for seaming and flashing. Rainy season can add 2–5 days of weather delays.

### Does a new metal roof increase home value in Broward County?

A new roof recoups **60–70%** of its cost at resale. But the bigger value in Broward's market is insurability — a home with a new metal roof is dramatically easier to sell because buyers can actually get insurance on it.

### Can I install a metal roof myself in Florida?

Florida's homeowner exemption allows it if you own and occupy the home. But you still need permits, inspections, and must meet all HVHZ code requirements. Insurance companies may refuse to cover a homeowner-installed roof. For most people, it's not worth the risk.

### Does homeowners insurance cover metal roof replacement?

Storm damage (wind, hail, fallen trees) is typically covered minus your deductible — often **2% of insured value** for hurricane claims in Florida. Normal wear and aging is never covered.

---

## Bottom Line

Installing a metal roof in Broward County runs **$25,000 to $55,000** for most homes, with 24-gauge standing seam at the sweet spot of cost and performance. The most important thing to know: Broward's HVHZ requirements add cost but your new roof meets some of the toughest wind standards in the country — and it keeps you insurable in a market where carriers are dropping homes with aging roofs every day. Get quotes from at least 3 licensed Florida roofing contractors and verify every license at MyFloridaLicense.com.
